meta.title: Battle of the Little Bighorn
meta.timeAnchor: Local apparent (sun) time per Gray (1991). Officer watch times (RCOI, Godfrey) run ~45–70 min ahead of this clock; offsets applied per research doc.

TERRY1876.citation: Annual Report of the Secretary of War 1876, p. 462

GRAHAM.citation: W.A. Graham, The Custer Myth / RCOI compilations

WIKI.citation: Wikipedia synthesis cited in the research dataset

HISTNET.citation: HistoryNet sources cited in the research dataset

WFHN.citation: Warfare History Network sources cited in the research dataset

WEB_MISC.citation: Forum/low-tier and other secondary sources cited in the research dataset; weak per D16

meta.notes: [CAL] values are engine parameters expected to move during calibration. [REVIEW] values await owner review. Disputes remain labeled and are never averaged.

provenance.note: D60 ruled coordinate. Prior APPROX coordinate: (45.42, -107.17). OSM basis: crowd-sourced MEDIUM-LOW provenance, OpenStreetMap natural=peak node 5486442915, 'Traditional Crows Nest?'. O3-A sweep corroboration: unique full-pass cell of 1,681; pony-herd ray CLEAR 26.98 km curvature-corrected; strongest pocket signature 91.8 m; 600 m from trail corridor; village ray BLOCKED from every cell. D27 marker-relocation lineage: the published NPS marker coordinates (45.52203, -107.37835) and (45.52595, -107.38372) are decoys, not the vantage. D60 reopen clause: field GPS or a specialist coordinate more than 1.5 km away triggers re-adjudication.

D27 marker-relocation: HMDB marker m=45587 is a roadside placement on the SE flank (45.5309, -107.3906; 1025 m, below Reno Hill); relocated ~300 m NNW to the DEM crest (1039 m), which commands Reno Hill as the plunging-fire accounts require. DEM + USGS EPQS cross-checked. Found by gate G2; second instance of the Crow's Nest-decoy pattern.
D53: old APPROX coordinate (45.52, -107.43) relocated at LOW to the D43 village-strip south-end midpoint (45.51833, -107.38873) for landmark/strip/order-objective coherence

Channel point nearest Reno Hill (460m); matches 'Reno's Retreat Crossing' bluff-base descriptions (GRI fig.11).
Modern channel via DEM thalweg; treated as 1876 meander-belt proxy per Martin 2010. Local 1876 deltas known at Garryowen loop and Ford B (flagged LOW zones).
Loop bend nearest valley fight + 250 m east-bank belt; extent approximated from channel geometry and accounts; Maguire 1876 map georeferencing = Tier B upgrade.
West-bank strip Garryowen->Ford B reach, 600 m wide, on terraces per GRI; length ~1 mi+ per Indian accounts.

ratingsProvenance.note: [REVIEW] Refused Gatling guns and the 2nd Cavalry battalion offer; applied the Washita template (divide & strike from multiple directions); attacked without full reconnaissance once he believed the command was spotted; operating assumption was that the danger was the village escaping, not defeating him.
[REVIEW] No prior experience against Plains warriors; halted the charge; timber stand then rout; possibly in shock on the hilltop.
[REVIEW] Documented defensive competence on the hilltop (organized perimeter, personally led a counterattack, hit in boot heel); but slow/deliberate return with packs.
[REVIEW] Held reserve on Battle Ridge; sector overwhelmed. Veteran captain.
[REVIEW] Led the left wing (E,F) to Ford B. Trusted subordinate.
[REVIEW] Held the Calhoun Hill skirmish line. Steady under pressure.
[REVIEW] Advanced to Weir Point (order vs. initiative disputed). Bold.
[REVIEW] Cleared jammed carbines on the hilltop line. Steady.
[REVIEW] Defended Reno at RCOI; competent company command. Reliable.
[REVIEW] TODO-AMBIGUOUS: research §H has no McDougall behavioral-evidence row; research §A identifies McDougall with B Company and the packs.
[REVIEW] TODO-AMBIGUOUS: research §H has no Sitting Bull row; research §B says: Spiritual/political leader; directed noncombatants north; not a field tactician.
[REVIEW] Led the Reno response, then the Calhoun assault. Aggressive flanker.
[REVIEW] Northern sweep, flanked Last Stand Hill. Brilliant, renowned.
[REVIEW] Rallied the decisive charge; killed at Deep Ravine. Decisive charge leader.
[REVIEW] TODO-AMBIGUOUS: research §H has no Hump row; research §B says: Keogh-sector assault.
